pattern: The Fibonacci property is purely local, so a run breaks exactly where a triple fails and a single sweep tracking the current valid run length suffices.
hints: Any window of length 1 or 2 already qualifies, so the answer is at least 2; the question is how far you can extend.
A triple of consecutive elements is valid only when the third equals the sum of the previous two; one bad triple ends the current run.
Sweep left to right keeping the length of the current Fibonacci run; when nums[i] equals nums[i-1] plus nums[i-2] grow it, otherwise restart the run at length 2.

edge_inputs.description: whole array is one Fibonacci run
all equal large values cap the run at 2
two separate runs, the later one is longer
near-miss where the sum is off by one

stress.description: mostly random with occasional planted Fibonacci runs to exercise both growth and reset
